title: Can France's right and center agree on a common presidential candidate?
subhead: While some supporters of President Emmanuel Macron have called for a joint primary with the right-wing Les Républicains, the main candidates have refused to take part, raising questions about whether their platforms are even compatible.

title: Macron adviser appointed culture minister in mini-reshuffle
Hillary Clinton calls for Trump to testify 'under oath' on Epstein before US House panel
Swedish military jams drone near French aircraft carrier
subhead: The drone was jammed about 13 kilometers from the Charles de Gaulle aircraft carrier. Later, Swedish Defence Minister Pal Jonson said the drone 'probably from Russia, as there was a Russian military vessel in the immediate vicinity.'

title: 'Europe's political landscape is shaped by two men who hold it in contempt: Trump and Putin'
subhead: The European Union recognizes the urgent need to achieve at least some strategic autonomy from a now hostile United States in order to confront the threat Russia poses to Europe, writes Le Monde columnist Alain Frachon.

title: 'The Epstein scandal is astonishing by the scale of the complicity and rape culture at the top of society'
subhead: The philosopher Marc Crépon, who specializes in the forms and effects of violence, analyzes the public response to the sex scandal in an interview with Le Monde. He warns against the risk of disregarding the suffering and trauma of the victims.

title: How Paris is turning into 'Frollywood,' a refuge for American actors
subhead: Whether George Clooney, Matt Dillon or Kristen Stewart, France is attracting more and more actors from across the Atlantic. This trend speaks to both the Trump-era crisis and the decline of the Hollywood model.

title: German court rules far-right AfD party cannot be labeled a 'confirmed extremist' group for now
French court sentences Iranian linked to prisoner swap to a year in prison
World Economic Forum CEO Brende resigns after Epstein links revealed
Paris mayor race: Center-right Bournazel defies calls for alliance with Dati
Park Chan-wook, director of 'No Other Choice,' still believes in the power of collective values
French culture minister Rachida Dati resigns to launch her Paris mayoral bid
Morocco's old colonial wounds reopened by tribute to French army auxiliaries
Hong Kong pro-democracy media mogul Jimmy Lai wins appeal in fraud case
'Women are much more frequently victims of physical violence at work than men'
After four years of war in Ukraine, Russia's economy could fall into recession
Swastikas spray-painted on former Jewish internment camp near Paris
Why Ethiopia and Eritrea are once again on the brink of war
The Langs' troubling friendship with Jeffrey Epstein
subhead: Former French culture minister Jack Lang and his daughter Caroline Lang appear thousands of times in the most recently released documents. All evidence suggests that she had a much closer relationship with the sex offender than she now admits.

title: Four years of war in Ukraine: Five photographers on assignment for Le Monde share their most striking images from 2025
subhead: Five photographers who covered the conflict in 2025 have each chosen an emblematic image of the conflict. Beyond the official figures and statements, these unique perspectives reveal the mark that this new year of war has left on them.

title: 'Trump is the president of instability and uncertainty, increasingly to his own detriment'
subhead: By continually flouting the rules, railing against institutions and single-handedly steering his country toward a new war, the president of the United States has demonstrated nothing but his inability to act as a statesman, observes Le Monde columnist Gilles Paris.

title: Mexico's endless battle against drug trafficking
